hero_subtitle: SFTY consolidates sanctions and adverse media screening results from multiple providers into a single operational workflow that streamlines review, case management, and audit evidence preservation.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance slows when screening outputs remain disconnected
problem_description: Sanctions and adverse media results often arrive from multiple providers in separate formats, creating manual reconciliation challenges for compliance teams. This fragmentation leads to inconsistent risk assessments, slower reviews, and a weaker audit trail across sanctions screening workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates sanctions and adverse media results from multiple providers into a unified compliance workflow that normalizes, routes, documents, and preserves audit evidence for faster, consistent reviews.
workflow_title: From screening outputs to compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY normalizes sanctions and adverse media screening results, routes alerts through review and case workflows, preserves evidence, and supports decision recording and reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented outputs. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into structured reviews, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing provider tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs by normalizing screening outputs into a controlled, audit-ready compliance layer optimized for bank due diligence and enterprise deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence preserves sanctions screening decisions

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions screening?
faq_1_answer: SFTY consolidates sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into a connected compliance workflow, enabling VASPs to manage alerts, reviews, cases and reporting efficiently within one system.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY normalize provider outputs?
faq_2_answer: SFTY standardizes and normalizes results from multiple screening providers to create consistent alerts, route reviews automatically, and open structured cases for operational efficiency and easier decision-making.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve screening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed evidence histories, decision logs and provider results to support audit readiness and support compliance teams can trace and explain sanctions-related decisions.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing screening tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with existing sanctions and adverse media screening providers without replacing them, layering a controlled operational workflow on top of their outputs.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above screening providers by connecting their outputs into structured workflows rather than replacing the underlying sanctions or adverse media tools.
final_title: Bring screening outputs into one compliance layer
final_description: Review how SFTY normalizes sanctions and adverse media results into structured cases, review workflows and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
